name: Monte Santa Trega and A Guarda Coastal Trail
name_original: A Guarda a Castro de Santa Trega — circular por el Monte Santa Trega y O Puntal
description: Hike the Monte Santa Trega and A Guarda Coastal Trail for panoramic views, ancient history at Castro de Santa Tecla, and rugged coastlines.
region_intro: Hiking around A Guarda offers diverse landscapes, from coastal paths along the Atlantic to elevated trails on Monte de Santa Trega. The region is characterized by its rugged coastline, the Miño River estuary forming a natural border, and hills providing panoramic views. Pine forests and historical sites, such as the Castro de Santa Trega, are integrated into the hiking experience. Best hiking trails around A Guarda The most popular hiking route is Monte Santa Trega and A Guarda Coastal Trail, a 7.4 miles (11.9 km) trail that takes 3 hours 31 minutes to complete. This moderate route combines coastal scenery with elevated views from Monte Santa Trega. Another top favourite among local hikers is Camiños do Trega Trail, a moderate 2.7 miles (4.3 km) path. This trail explores Monte Santa Trega, providing access to its archaeological site and panoramic vistas. Local hikers also love the A Guarda Coastal Trail, a 2.2 miles (3.6 km) trail leading through coastal scenery, often completed in about 1 hour. Hiking around A Guarda is defined by coastal paths, mountainous terrain, and views of the Miño River estuary. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy coastal walks to moderate hill climbs. The routes in A Guarda are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 700 hikers have used komoot to explore A Guarda's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in A Guarda?
A: A Guarda offers a wide variety of hiking experiences, with over 35 trails recorded on komoot. These routes cater to different preferences, including easy coastal walks and more challenging climbs up Monte de Santa Trega.

Q: What types of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in A Guarda?
A: Hiking in A Guarda provides diverse scenery, from rugged Atlantic coastlines and the panoramic views of the Miño River estuary to the elevated terrain of Monte de Santa Trega. You'll also find trails winding through pine forests and past historical sites.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in A Guarda?
A: A Guarda is a great destination for hiking throughout much of the year. The region benefits from a mild climate, making spring and autumn particularly pleasant for exploring. Summer offers warm weather for coastal walks, while winter can still be suitable for hiking, though some trails might be wetter.

Q: Are there options for easy hikes in A Guarda?
A: Yes, A Guarda has plenty of easy hiking options. Approximately 20 of the trails are rated as easy, perfect for a relaxed stroll. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991655318/a-guarda-coastal-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;A Guarda Coastal Trail&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 2.2-mile (3.6 km) path along the scenic coastline.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in A Guarda?
A: The hiking trails in A Guarda are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.4 stars from over 100 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the stunning coastal views, the historical significance of sites like Castro de Santa Trega, and the diverse landscapes.

Q: Can I find trails with significant viewpoints in A Guarda?
A: Absolutely. Monte de Santa Trega is a prime location for breathtaking views, offering panoramic vistas of the Miño River estuary, the Atlantic Ocean, and even Portugal. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1375194170/monte-santa-trega-and-a-guarda-coastal-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte Santa Trega and A Guarda Coastal Trail&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent choice for combining elevated views with coastal scenery.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in A Guarda?
A: Many of the easier trails in A Guarda are suitable for families. The coastal paths and some of the shorter routes on Monte de Santa Trega, like sections of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991647101/caminos-do-trega-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camiños do Trega Trail&lt;/a&gt;, offer manageable distances and interesting sights for all ages, including historical ruins.

Q: Are dogs allowed on hiking trails in A Guarda?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on many hiking trails in A Guarda, especially in natural areas. However, it's always recommended to keep them on a leash, particularly in protected areas or near archaeological sites like Castro de Santa Trega, and to clean up after them.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in A Guarda?
A: Yes, A Guarda offers several circular routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6950810" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castrexo Pine Forest – A Guarda loop&lt;/a&gt; is a great example, allowing you to explore the pine forest and return to your starting point without retracing your steps.

Q: What historical sites can I explore while hiking in A Guarda?
A: A Guarda is rich in history. While hiking, you can visit the ancient Castrexo-Roman settlement of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5326788" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castro de Santa Trega&lt;/a&gt; on Monte de Santa Trega, which dates back to the 4th century BC. You might also encounter petroglyphs and the 12th-century hermitage of Santa Tecla.

Q: Are there any waterfalls or natural pools near the hiking trails?
A: Yes, for those seeking natural water features, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3124664" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozas de Loureza Natural Pools and Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt; are a notable attraction in the vicinity of A Guarda's hiking trails, offering a refreshing stop.

Q: Is public transport available to reach hiking trailheads in A Guarda?
A: Public transport options to reach certain trailheads, particularly those around Monte de Santa Trega, may be available. It's advisable to check local bus schedules for routes connecting A Guarda town to key hiking areas, though some more remote starting points might require private transport.

Q: Where can I find parking for hiking trails in A Guarda?
A: Parking is generally available at popular hiking destinations. For trails on Monte de Santa Trega, there are designated parking areas. For coastal and forest trails, you can often find parking in or near A Guarda town or at specific trail access points. Always look for marked parking zones.
tips: The castros were fortified enclosures, surrounded by moats and walls. Inside, the stone houses, with circular layouts and thatched roofs, were distributed in neighbourhoods, separated by streets and squares. Other longer constructions functioned as meeting or worship spaces, and some villages even had steam baths. The Castro de Santa Tecla, in the Galician town of A Guarda (Pontevedra), is a representative example, but there are more.

A small mountain that hosted one of the largest Celtic settlements in the northeast of the Peninsula. Mount Santa Tecla –Santa Trega, in Galician– and the fort of the same name. The Santa Tecla castro is the most emblematic and visited in Galicia and it is not surprising at all. A relatively well preserved citania –pre-Roman fortified city–, a small museum with unique pieces and sensational views.
A beautiful beach on the Miño estuary with views of Portugal on the other side of the river.
The southernmost tip of Galicia and border with Portugal where the Miño River flows into the Atlantic Ocean. Beautiful walk along a wooden path surrounded by sea pines.
Walking towards A Guarda from the south, the view of the town and the bay opens up in front of you with Monte Torroso rising in the background.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Monte Santa Trega and A Guarda Coastal Trail?
faqs.answer: You can typically find parking in the town of A Guarda, which serves as a common starting point for this trail. From there, you can begin your hike towards Monte Santa Trega and the coastal sections.

faqs.question: Is the Monte Santa Trega and A Guarda Coastal Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in populated areas, near historical sites like Castro de Santa Tecla, and to be mindful of local regulations and other hikers.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed year-round, but spring and autumn often offer the most pleasant hiking conditions with milder temperatures and fewer crowds. Summer can be warm, especially on exposed coastal sections, while winter might bring more unpredictable weather.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail?
faqs.answer: While the general coastal trail does not require permits, there might be a small entrance fee to access the archaeological site of Castro de Santa Tecla and the museum (MASAT) located on Monte de Santa Trega itself. The hiking paths around the mountain are generally free to access.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the Monte Santa Trega and A Guarda Coastal Trail?
faqs.answer: The trail offers diverse terrain. You'll encounter coastal paths, some sections of road walking (including tarmac), and occasional forest paths, including eucalyptus forests. On Monte de Santa Trega, there's a network of nature trails, some paved, with varying inclines leading to its summit.

faqs.question: What are the main highlights along the route?
faqs.answer: This trail is rich in highlights! You can explore the ancient Castro de Santa Tecla, enjoy panoramic views from Monte de Santa Trega, relax at Armona Beach, discover the unique Painted Trees at O Puntal, and take in the stunning View of A Guarda and Monte Torroso. You'll also pass through charming fishing villages and historical sites.

faqs.question: Is the trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: The overall difficulty is rated as moderate, but many sections, especially along the coast, are considered easy and suitable for most fitness levels. The ascent of Monte de Santa Trega involves some inclines but is rewarding for its views. Beginners can choose to focus on the flatter coastal parts or the shorter, paved trails on the mountain.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other hiking routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, this trail is an integral part of the Portuguese Coastal Way (Caminho Português da Costa), offering a quieter alternative for pilgrims. It also intersects with sections of the Senda Litoral de (A Guarda), [CIMA PO01] Monte de Santa Tegra, and Viacrucis.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or find water along the trail?
faqs.answer: You'll find amenities like cafes and restaurants in the town of A Guarda. Along some coastal sections, services might be sparse, so it's advisable to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially for longer stretches between villages.

faqs.question: What makes this trail a unique hiking experience?
faqs.answer: This trail stands out for its blend of spectacular coastal scenery and profound historical significance. It offers breathtaking panoramic views of the Miño River Estuary and the Atlantic, combined with the chance to explore the ancient Castro de Santa Tecla, one of Galicia's most important Celtic hill forts. It also forms a visually rich section of the Portuguese Coastal Way.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail or out-and-back?
faqs.answer: The specific Komoot route for the Monte Santa Trega and A Guarda Coastal Trail is designed as a circular route, allowing you to experience both the mountain and coastal sections without retracing your steps entirely.

waypoints.name: View of A Guarda and Monte Torroso
